feed.title: Onion Message Jamming in the Lightning Network

entry.title: Onion Message Jamming in the Lightning Network
entry.summary:
The discussion centers around the complexities and potential benefits of a two-layer payment system in environments where anonymity or pseudonymity is essential, such as in decentralized online services. This system separates the purchase event from the actual payment process using ecash credentials. Such a separation is deemed crucial for enhancing flexibility in cryptographic methods specifically tailored to address these unique challenges. Unlike the cryptographic primitives inherited from Bitcoin and utilized in Lightning Network (LN), which may not be ideally suited for these scenarios, the proposed method could offer a more fitting solution.
The intricacy of this approach, particularly concerning the economic relationships at each node, might initially appear daunting. However, it has been argued that this complexity is less burdensome than it first seems. This perspective suggests an optimization of the current systems in place, proposing that while the set-up might look complex, its operational demands could be more manageable than anticipated. This nuanced view highlights a strategic adaptation of technology to better meet the specific needs of secure, anonymous transactions in digital spaces.
